problem: 86% of cron jobs use identical model (openrouter/z-ai/glm-4.5-air:free)
rule: No single model may power >75% of cron jobs. Implement model diversity mandate for critical infrastructure.
enforcement: Tag cron jobs by priority. Critical jobs must use different models. Auto-rebalance if threshold exceeded.

problem: 100% of cron jobs depend on single provider (OpenRouter)
rule: No single provider may be sole inference path for >75% of cron jobs. Maintain direct API fallbacks.
enforcement: Add at least 2 direct provider configs (Anthropic, Google). Test failover quarterly.

problem: 100% of cron jobs use identical session target ('isolated')
rule: No single session target type may exceed 75% of cron jobs. Mix main/isolated targets for resilience.
enforcement: Reclassify 25% of non-critical crons to use 'main' session target. Monitor availability.

problem: Clean bill - No model provider dominance (10/10 LLM providers active)
rule: Maintain current provider diversity. Add new providers when available.
enforcement: Quarterly audit of LLM provider landscape. Add new entrants within 90 days of launch.

problem: Clean bill - No skill authorship concentration (67 skill directories with diverse authors)
rule: Maintain current skill diversity. Continue external contribution strategy.
enforcement: Monthly ClawHub integration check. Install at least 1 new community skill quarterly.

problem: Directory structure suggests potential ownership concentration
rule: Cap maximum percentage at 60%; implement progressive redistribution for shares >60%

problem: Telegram handles 100% of all human-agent communication (single channel dependency)
rule: Maintain at least 2 active communication channels. Add Discord or email fallback.
enforcement: If Telegram goes down, agency is unreachable. Enable at least one backup channel.

problem: 100% of workspace skills (61/61) authored by single agency with no external contributors
rule: Seek at least 2 external skill contributions or ClawHub installs to break authorship monopoly.
enforcement: Quarterly ClawHub diversity audit. Install at least 3 community skills.

problem: 36% of cron jobs in error state due to free-tier rate limits — dependency lock on free models
rule: Free-tier models may not power >40% of cron jobs. Mix paid-tier models for critical crons.
enforcement: Tag crons as critical/non-critical. Critical crons must use paid-tier or self-hosted models.

problem: 100% of main-session inference routes through single proxy (api.ampere.sh)
rule: Maintain a direct Anthropic API fallback. No single proxy may be sole inference path.
enforcement: Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY with direct api.anthropic.com as secondary route. Test monthly.

problem: OpenRouter routes 62% of configured models (8/13) — approaching colonizer threshold
rule: No single routing layer may serve >60% of available models. Add direct provider endpoints for Google, xAI, DeepSeek.
enforcement: Add at least 2 direct-provider model configs to reduce OpenRouter dependency. Monitor quarterly.

problem: Directory structure suggests potential ownership concentration
rule: Cap maximum percentage at 60%; implement progressive redistribution for shares >60%
